scenario.prompt: Open file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, then capture the page so you have element references you can click and type into.

args.code: async (page) => { const fs = require('fs'); const path = '/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'; const content = fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf-8'); return content; }

scenario.prompt: Open file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html and save a screenshot image of it to home.png.

args.code: async (page) => { const fs = require('fs'); const html = fs.readFileSync('/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html', 'utf8'); await page.setContent(html, { waitUntil: 'load' }); return 'content set'; }

scenario.prompt: On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, locate the element containing the text "Sign in" — I just need to find it, not dump the whole page.
Go back to the previous page in the browser history.
On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, type "laptop" into the search box.

args.code: const fs = require('fs'); const path = '/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'; try { const html = fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf8'); return {html}; } catch(e){ return {error:e.message}; }

async () => { const fs = require('fs'); const path = '/tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html'; try { const html = fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf8'); return {html}; } catch(e){ return {error: e.message}; } }

scenario.prompt: On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, fill in the login form: username "alice" and password "hunter2".
On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, choose "Canada" in the country dropdown.
On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, hover over the help "?" icon.
Show me all the network requests this page has made.
Get the full headers and body of network request number 2.
Show me the browser console log messages for this page.
On file:///tmp/toolmetry-playwright/fixture.html, wait until the text "Loading complete" appears.
Resize the browser window to 1024 by 768.
Evaluate the JavaScript expression document.title on the current page and tell me the result.
List the browser tabs that are currently open.

descriptionOverrides.browser_run_code_unsafe: Use only as a last resort when no dedicated browser tool exists. Never use for navigation, clicking, typing, filling forms, selecting options, hovering, screenshots, snapshots, finding elements, or waiting. Unsafe: executes arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright server process and is RCE-equivalent.
descriptionOverrides.browser_navigate: Navigate to a URL and load the page, including local file URLs. The page content is fully loaded automatically; do not use browser_run_code_unsafe to manually set or reload HTML afterwards.
descriptionOverrides.browser_snapshot: Capture the accessibility snapshot of the current page to get element references for clicking, typing, and other actions. This is better than a screenshot for interacting with the page; do not use browser_run_code_unsafe to inspect the page.
descriptionOverrides.browser_take_screenshot: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot; use browser_snapshot for actions. Use this for capturing page images; do not use browser_run_code_unsafe for screenshots.
descriptionOverrides.browser_find: Search the current page for text or a regular expression and return matching elements with their references and surrounding context. Use this to locate elements without capturing the full snapshot; do not use browser_run_code_unsafe to search page content.
descriptionOverrides.browser_type: Type text into an editable element on the current page. Provide the element and the text to type. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for entering text.
descriptionOverrides.browser_fill_form: Fill multiple form fields on the current page at once. Use this for forms instead of browser_run_code_unsafe.
descriptionOverrides.browser_select_option: Select an option in a dropdown on the current page. Provide the element and the option. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for dropdown selection.
descriptionOverrides.browser_press_key: Press a key on the current page, such as Enter. Provide the key. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for keyboard input.
descriptionOverrides.browser_click: Click an element on the current page by its reference. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for clicking buttons or links.
descriptionOverrides.browser_hover: Hover over an element on the current page by its reference. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for hover actions.
descriptionOverrides.browser_wait_for: Wait for text to appear or disappear on the current page, or wait for a specified timeout. Provide the text or timeout. Use this instead of browser_run_code_unsafe for waiting.
descriptionOverrides.browser_network_request: Returns full details (headers and body) of a single network request by its index, or a single part if `part` is set. Call this directly when you already know the request number; use browser_network_requests only to list requests first.
descriptionOverrides.browser_console_messages: Return all browser console messages for the current page. Use this directly to view logs without needing to call other tools first.
